1_when_to_use_matrix.principle: Matrix needed when multiple sources must be independently routed to multiple zones
scenarios: Hotel: lobby music, restaurant music, pool music, conference audio — different source per zone
Airport: gate announcements, general BGM, emergency — priority routing
Shopping mall: zone-specific music + central paging
School: bell schedule, classroom PA, playground, admin announcements

2_matrix_sizing.principle: Matrix inputs ≥ audio sources, matrix outputs ≥ speaker zones

guide.best_for: Medium installations: hotel, small mall, school
Large installations: airport, hospital, large campus

4_zone_amplifier_connection.principle: Each matrix output connects to a zone amplifier which drives speakers
4_zone_amplifier_connection.chain: Audio Source → Matrix Input → Matrix Routing → Matrix Output → Zone Amplifier → 100V Speakers
amplifier_options: MAG6812-MAG6865 (network zone terminal with built-in amp)

5_priority_management.principle: Emergency messages override all other sources automatically
priority_levels: 1: Fire/Emergency (highest — from fire panel via EMC)

examples: Morning: calm music in lobby, energetic in gym
Afternoon: prayer call in mosque zones, BGM elsewhere
Evening: dim music in restaurant, upbeat in bar
School: bells at class change, BGM during breaks

options: PC software via RS-485 or TCP/IP

8_expansion.principle: Matrix can be expanded with D6223/D6224 zone expanders
8_expansion.rule: Plan for 20% spare inputs and outputs for future sources and zones

9_integration_with_ip_pa.principle: Matrix can coexist with or feed into IP PA network
9_integration_with_ip_pa.method: MAG6588 outputs → MAG network terminals OR traditional amplifiers. This creates a hybrid traditional+IP architecture.
